seoTitle: How to Verify and Link Your Bank Account for eBay Payouts
metaDescription: Learn how to verify and link a bank account on eBay for seller payouts, including the micro-deposit process and fixes for common linking errors.
intro: Getting paid for your eBay sales starts with linking and verifying a bank account in your seller payment settings. This guide walks through adding your bank details, completing eBay's micro-deposit verification, and fixing the most common errors sellers run into. It works whether you're setting up payouts for the first time or switching to a different bank like Chase, Bank of America, or Wells Fargo.

steps.heading: Open your eBay payment settings
steps.body: Go to your account's Payments section in Seller Hub, where you manage how eBay sends your sale proceeds.

steps.heading: Add your bank account details
steps.body: Enter your bank's routing and account number to link the checking account you want payouts sent to.

steps.heading: Complete micro-deposit verification
steps.body: eBay sends small test deposits to confirm you own the account; check your bank statement and enter the amounts back into eBay to verify.

steps.heading: Fix common linking errors
steps.body: If verification fails, double-check the account and routing numbers, confirm the account is a checking account, and retry, since most errors come from typos or unsupported account types.

steps.heading: Update your bank details later if needed
steps.body: Return to the same Payments settings anytime to swap in a new bank account when you switch banks.

faq.question: How long does eBay bank verification take?
faq.answer: Micro-deposit verification typically takes a couple of business days for the test deposits to appear in your bank account.

faq.question: Why does eBay keep rejecting my bank account?
faq.answer: This is usually due to a mismatched account name, an unsupported account type, or an incorrect routing number entered during setup.

faq.question: Can I use eBay with any bank?
faq.answer: eBay supports most major U.S. banks, including Bank of America, Chase, and Wells Fargo, as long as the account is a standard checking account.

faq.question: Can I change my bank account on eBay after it's verified?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update your linked bank account at any time from the same payment settings, though you'll need to verify the new account again.
